title: croton costaricensis
intro: Croton costaricensis, also known as the Costarican croton, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae) native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Croton costaricensis include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to treat respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent such as ethanol.
